title: T-026 — API: finite-capacity scheduling stub (greedy)
prompt:
Implement scheduling stub per PRD §11.1 (FR-PLAN-030):
1. Line/machine availability check (is_active + shift calendar from 02-SETTINGS §12 + existing WO bookings).
2. Capacity tally per line per scheduling window.
3. Greedy first-fit: WOs sorted by priority DESC, scheduled_start_time ASC.
4. Overflow flag `scheduled_slot_conflict=true` (no block).
5. DAG respect: downstream WO scheduled_start_time >= parent.projected_end_time.

Populates wo.scheduled_start_time + scheduled_end_time. Outbox event 'wo.scheduled' (§11.4).

RED test asserts greedy allocation order, conflict flag on overflow, DAG ordering preserved.

pipeline_inputs.description: Greedy first-fit scheduling stub with DAG ordering respect and overflow flagging.
pipeline_inputs.details: Per WO duration = SUM(wo_operations.expected_duration_minutes). Shift calendar query reads 02-SETTINGS §12 production_shifts table. Conflict flag stored on wo (NOT separate table). Stub does NOT preempt or re-optimize — full engine in 07-PLANNING-EXT (§11.3 limitations).
acceptance_criteria: Given 3 WOs sorted by priority [critical=1, normal=2, low=3] on a single line, when scheduler runs, then scheduled_start_time order is [critical, normal, low].
Given line capacity 8h and 3 WOs of 4h each, when scheduler runs, then the third WO has scheduled_slot_conflict=true.
Given a parent WO with projected_end_time T+60min, when child WO scheduled, then child.scheduled_start_time >= T+60min.
Given `pnpm vitest run src/server/services/__tests__/scheduling-stub.test.ts`, when run, then 0 failures.
test_strategy: RED tests for priority ordering, capacity overflow flag, DAG ordering.
risk_red_lines: Do not preempt or re-optimize — §11.3 limitations apply to P1.
Do not block on overflow — flag only.
